answer:
Honestly, my closet kind of reflects who I am right now, you know? As an 18-year-old who just graduated high school and is applying to universities in the U.S., there’s a mix of stuff—some casual clothes I wear every day, like hoodies and jeans, and then there are a couple of nicer shirts I save for interviews or special occasions. I guess you could say my closet is organized chaos. I mean, it’s not super neat, but everything has its place, which sort of matches how my life feels with all the changes happening.

One thing that really stands out is my collection of sneakers. I have maybe five pairs, each with a story—like the ones I wore to prom, or the pair I got after finishing my college applications. These shoes sort of remind me of different milestones, so they’re more than just something to wear; they’re connected to my memories and personal growth over the past year.

There’s also a small shelf with some books and a camera I use for photography, which shows I’m someone who enjoys creativity and capturing moments. My closet isn’t just about clothes—it’s like a snapshot of my interests.

If I could add one item next year, honestly I’d like to get a really good blazer. I feel like, moving into university, having one would make me look more professional for important events, job interviews, or presentations. Plus, it would definitely boost my confidence. So, yeah, my closet says I’m in transition, and the blazer would symbolize taking the next step toward adulthood.
title: Describe the inside of your closet. What do the contents say about your life? What is one item you want to add to your closet in the next year and why?
